claim_boundary: This contract converts C-MAPSS published-baseline comparison into a field-by-field reproduction checklist. It does not open the official SOTA gate until a published baseline has exact source-matched preprocessing, budget, metrics, and archived predictions.

source_registry.title: NASA CMAPSS Jet Engine Simulated Data
source_registry.contract_use: Authoritative native task, split, and terminal-test RUL source.

source_registry.title: Downloaded NASA C-MAPSS readme.txt
source_registry.contract_use: Local checksum-level evidence that the ready data follows the NASA package description.

source_registry.title: Damage Propagation Modeling for Aircraft Engine Run-to-Failure Simulation
source_registry.contract_use: Dataset lineage and benchmark citation, not a neural baseline protocol.

source_registry.title: Deep convolutional neural network based regression approach for estimation of remaining useful life
confirmed_fields: temporal filters over multi-channel sensor data
source_registry.contract_use: Candidate published CNN baseline family; exact preprocessing and budget must still be reproduced.

source_registry.title: Long Short-Term Memory Network for Remaining Useful Life estimation
source_registry.contract_use: Highest-priority exact reproduction target because a local LSTM runner already exists.

source_registry.title: Remaining Useful Life Prediction for Aero-Engines Based on Multi-Scale Dilated Fusion Attention Model
confirmed_fields: window=30, batch=32, epochs=100, Adam, lr=0.0001, dropout=0.3
dilation rates 1/2/4 with channel and spatial attention
source_registry.contract_use: Open full-text P0 exact-source target after PCA/key-sensor preprocessing and budget equivalence are verified.

source_registry.title: Remaining Useful Life Prediction Using Attention-LSTM Neural Network of Aircraft Engines
source_registry.contract_use: Recent high-performing subset-limited reference; useful for FD001/FD003 discussion, not FD001-FD004 all-subset proof.

source_registry.title: Remaining Useful Life Estimation of Turbofan Engines with Deep Learning Using Change-Point Detection Based Labeling and Feature Engineering
source_registry.contract_use: Useful FD001 preprocessing reference; not sufficient for all-subset official SOTA wording.

required_reproduction_fields.description: NASA FD001-FD004 train files, test files, and terminal RUL files are used without regrouping test engines.
The compared baseline declares exactly which FD subsets are evaluated, and all reported tables use the same subset scope.
RUL target construction is explicit, including any piecewise cap, early-life clipping, or train-unit pseudo-terminal validation policy.
Selected operational settings and sensors are listed; dropped constant or non-informative channels are reproducible.
Scaler, regime normalizer, or filtering parameters are fit only on training data and reused for validation/test.
Window length, stride, padding/truncation, and last-cycle terminal evaluation are identical across compared methods.
Layer count, hidden/channel sizes, attention heads, kernels, dropout, and parameter count are declared.
Epochs, batch size, optimizer, learning rate, early stopping, hardware device, and seeds are budget-matched.
RMSE and PHM-style asymmetric RUL score are recomputed from archived per-test-engine predictions.
Each seed stores per-test-engine prediction records with subset, unit id, true RUL, predicted RUL, and split role.

known_not_exact_reasons: exact hidden size, layer count, preprocessing, validation split, and budget are not proven to match the paper
no exact local CNN reproduction artifact exists
source preprocessing, windowing, and training budget are not encoded in the current runner
source_2d runner and local branch archives exist, but the best branch changes window/dropout/condition preprocessing relative to the MDFA table
Table-4 key-sensor PCA is machine-readable but performs poorly in a short-budget probe, especially on multi-condition subsets
exact cumulative-contribution threshold and budget equivalence are not yet verified against the source
published result covers FD001 and FD003, not all FD001-FD004 subsets
no local attention-LSTM exact reproduction artifact exists
FD001-only study cannot prove FD001-FD004 all-subset alignment
change-point label and filter details are not implemented in the current exact-native runner

next_reproduction_queue.action: Promote local lstm_sequence from published-style to exact reproduction by matching the paper preprocessing, sequence length, hidden/layer config, epochs, validation policy, and per-subset table.
Use cmapss_lstm_source_protocol_audit to keep the local LSTM row blocked until primary full-text protocol fields are available and verified.
Use cmapss_mdfa_source_profile and the Table-4 key-sensor probe to separate exact-source reproduction from the stronger local branch before any gate flip.
Implement the temporal CNN baseline only after exact sensor/window/preprocessing and training budget fields are extracted from the source paper.
Use only as an FD001/FD003 subset reference unless a full FD001-FD004 compatible reproduction is created.

paper_policy: Keep C-MAPSS wording at original-task transfer evidence. Do not claim literature-wide RUL SOTA; do not compare the pooled FD001-FD004 RMSE directly against FD001-only or FD001/FD003-only references.
